Today is February 10, 2026. Regarding the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, what specific action did the FBI observe the masked individual performing in the newly recovered surveillance footage?
(A) Picking up vegetation from the ground to cover the camera lens
(B) Using a specialized tool to disconnect the doorbell's power supply
(C) Spraying a dark substance directly onto the lens of the Nest camera
(D) Removing the recording device from the door frame entirely
(E) Placing a piece of adhesive tape over the motion sensor
explanation: According to the article, the FBI released videos showing the person approach the front door, check the camera, and then pick up "some vegetation from the ground and using it to cover the camera lens."
model_output:
In the newly recovered surveillance footage, the FBI saw the masked individual **covering the camera lens with their gloved hand and then using plants** from Nancy Guthrie’s yard to obscure the camera. ([yahoo.com](https://www.yahoo.com/news/articles/fbi-releases-photos-masked-man-180554510.html/?utm_source=openai))

So, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>  
(C)  
</final_answer>

Today is February 10, 2026. According to the FBI summary of the 2019 interview with Michael Reiter, what specific description did Donald Trump allegedly use for Ghislaine Maxwell during a 2006 phone call?
(A) A disgusting creep
(B) A professional recruiter
(C) A dangerous associate
(D) An evil operative
(E) A disgusting operative
explanation: The document claims Reiter said Trump told him Ghislaine Maxwell was Epstein's "operative" and that "she is evil."
model_output:
According to the FBI summary of the 2019 interview with Michael Reiter, in the 2006 phone call Donald Trump allegedly described Ghislaine Maxwell as “evil” and told the chief to “focus on her”—also calling her Epstein’s “operative.” ([nypost.com](https://nypost.com/2026/02/10/us-news/trump-told-palm-beach-police-chief-thank-goodness-youre-stopping-epstein-claimed-ghislaine-maxwell-was-evil-in-2006-phone-call-fbi-document/?utm_source=openai))

The option that matches this description is:

(E) A disgusting operative — incorrect  
(B) A professional recruiter — incorrect  
(C) A dangerous associate — incorrect  
(D) An evil operative — correct  

So, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- New York Post (“Trump told Palm Beach police chief … called Ghislaine Maxwell ‘evil’ …”) ([nypost.com](https://nypost.com/2026/02/10/us-news/trump-told-palm-beach-police-chief-thank-goodness-youre-stopping-epstein-claimed-ghislaine-maxwell-was-evil-in-2006-phone-call-fbi-document/?utm_source=openai))  
- NDTV Profit (“Trump described Maxwell as Epstein’s ‘operative’ … ‘she is evil and to focus on her.’”) ([ndtvprofit.com](https://www.ndtvprofit.com/world/039-evil-039-039-disgusting-039-trump-bashed-epstein-ghislaine-maxwell-during-first-probe-records-show-10982038/amp/1?utm_source=openai))

Today is February 10, 2026. What is the reported production budget per episode for the Canadian television show "Heated Rivalry"?
(A) C$10m
(B) C$3.6m
(C) C$2.6m
(D) C$5m
(E) C$1.5m
explanation: The article states the show was "produced in Canada on a minimal budget - reportedly less than C$5m ($3.6m; £2.6m) per episode."
model_output:
From various reports, the production budget for *Heated Rivalry* was stated to be **less than CAD $5 million** per episode. Specifically, it was described as “reportedly less than C$5m ($3.6m …) per episode” and also mentioned as “a budget set to just under 3 million CAD (2.2 million USD) per episode.” ([aol.com](https://www.aol.com/news/no-big-names-no-big-002328874.html?utm_source=openai))

Among the options given:

(A) C$10m — too high  
(B) C$3.6m — matches the “less than C$5m ($3.6m …)” figure  
(C) C$2.6m — doesn’t align with reported figures  
(D) C$5m — this is the upper bound, but reports say “less than” so not exactly C$5m  
(E) C$1.5m — too low  

The best fit is:

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [AOL “No big names. No big budget. But Heated Rivalry is a big hit”] (https://www.aol.com/news/no-big-names-no-big-002328874.html)  
- [Wikipedia “Heated Rivalry” page] (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heated_Rivalry)  
- [Los Angeles Times “How ‘Heated Rivalry’ became a joyful community”] (https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/awards/story/2026-02-03/how-heated-rivalry-changed-game-for-canadian-tv)

Today is February 10, 2026. According to Pima County officials, at what specific time did the app on Nancy Guthrie's pacemaker disconnect from her phone?
(A) 02:28
(B) 01:47
(C) 02:12
(D) 03:00
(E) 01:15
explanation: The article states: "At 02:28, the app on her pacemaker, an implanted cardiac device, disconnected from her phone."
model_output:
The correct answer is:

<final_answer>  
(A)  
</final_answer>

Here’s how it matches the timeline according to Pima County officials:

- The doorbell camera disconnected at **1:47 a.m.** ([people.com](https://people.com/timeline-savannah-guthrie-mom-disappearance-shared-by-police-11900602?utm_source=openai))  
- The pacemaker app disconnected from her phone at **2:28 a.m.** ([people.com](https://people.com/timeline-savannah-guthrie-mom-disappearance-shared-by-police-11900602?utm_source=openai))

So the specific time when the pacemaker app disconnected was **02:28**.

Today is February 10, 2026. Which specific consumer category saw a 0.9% month-over-month decline in sales at the end of 2025?
(A) Clothing retailers
(B) Electronics
(C) Furniture stores
(D) Gasoline
(E) Building materials
explanation: The article states: "Furniture store sales fell 0.9% month-over-month, while sales at clothing retailers declined 0.7%."
model_output:
The correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

Reasoning:

- In December 2025, the furniture and home furnishings category saw a month-over-month drop of about **0.9%** compared with November. ([hometextilestoday.com](https://www.hometextilestoday.com/furniture-retailing/copy-of-home-furnishings-stores-see-sharp-decline-in-december-says-doc/?utm_source=openai))
- Other categories like clothing and electronics declined, too, but by smaller amounts: clothing retail saw a drop of about 0.7%, electronics about 0.4%. ([kpmg.com](https://kpmg.com/us/en/articles/2026/december-2025-retail-sales.html?utm_source=openai))

So the specific consumer category was (C) Furniture stores.

Today is February 10, 2026. According to the White House and President Trump, in what year did Trump break off contact with Jeffrey Epstein?
(A) 2006
(B) 1990
(C) 2008
(D) 2004
(E) 2019
explanation: The article states: "the president and the White House have repeatedly said he was in the dark about Epstein's crimes before he broke off contact with him in around 2004..."
model_output:
Based on statements from President Trump and the White House in 2019, Trump claimed he had "had a falling out with [Epstein] a long time ago" and that he “hadn’t spoken to him for 15 years.” ([time.com](https://time.com/7333327/new-jeffrey-epstein-emails-allege-trump-aware-of-conduct/?utm_source=openai))  

If he said that in 2019, 15 years earlier would be **2004**. ([washingtonpost.com](https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2025/07/24/trump-jeffrey-epstein-relationship-controversy-timeline//?utm_source=openai))  

Therefore, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Time, *What the New Epstein Files Say About Trump: Updates* ([time.com](https://time.com/7333327/new-jeffrey-epstein-emails-allege-trump-aware-of-conduct/?utm_source=openai))  
- PBS NewsHour, *The facts and timeline of Trump and Epstein’s falling out* ([pbs.org](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/the-facts-and-timeline-of-trump-and-epsteins-falling-out?utm_source=openai))

Today is February 10, 2026. What is the name of the proposed legislation aimed at removing the time limit for survivors of sexual abuse to file civil claims?
(A) Giuffre's Act
(B) The Epstein Transparency Act
(C) The Survivors Justice Bill
(D) The Roberts Law
(E) Virginia's Law
explanation: The article mentions survivors gathered to announce "Virginia's Law, legislation that aims to remove the time limit for survivors of sexual abuse to file civil claims."
model_output:
The correct answer is:

<final_answer>  
(C)  
</final_answer>

**Reasoning:**

The proposed legislation is called the **“Virginia Giuffre’s Survivors’ Act”**, aiming to remove the statute of limitations for survivors of sex trafficking and related abuse claims. ([thetimes.com](https://www.thetimes.com/us/american-politics/article/virginias-law-epstein-victims-sgv36bkjq?utm_source=openai))

Out of the given options:

- (A) Giuffre’s Act — Close, but the actual name is **Virginia Giuffre’s Survivors’ Act** ([thetimes.com](https://www.thetimes.com/us/american-politics/article/virginias-law-epstein-victims-sgv36bkjq?utm_source=openai))  
- (B), (D), (E) are not relevant or correct  
- (C) The Survivors Justice Bill — This most closely matches the general purpose of the proposal, but isn't the exact name either

Since none of the options exactly match "Virginia Giuffre’s Survivors’ Act," the best fit given the choices is (C), referring to a survivors-focused justice bill aimed at removing time limits.

Today is February 10, 2026. For how long has Ilia Malinin remained unbeaten in figure skating competition?
(A) Almost two and a half years
(B) Since the 2022 Olympics
(C) Exactly three years
(D) Five years
(E) One year
explanation: The article states: "He has not been beaten in competition for almost two and a half years."
model_output:
Ilia Malinin has been unbeaten in competition for **almost two and a half years**. ([nbcconnecticut.com](https://www.nbcconnecticut.com/olympics/2026-milan-cortina/ilia-malinin-4th-consecutive-usa-figure-skating-title/3685647/?utm_source=openai))

Here’s how that conclusion is reached:

- His most recent loss was to Adam Siao Him Fa at the Grand Prix de France in early November 2023. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_Grand_Prix_de_France?utm_source=openai))  
- Since that event, he has won every competition through Olympic trials and national championships up to February 2026. ([nbcconnecticut.com](https://www.nbcconnecticut.com/olympics/2026-milan-cortina/ilia-malinin-4th-consecutive-usa-figure-skating-title/3685647/?utm_source=openai))  
- From November 2023 to February 2026 is about 2 years and 3 months — hence “almost two and a half years.”

Therefore, the correct answer is:  
<final_answer>  
(A)  
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 10, 2026. Who is the leader of the band of Buddhist monks completing the "Walk of Peace"?
(A) Mariann Budde
(B) Huong Dao
(C) Vipassana Bhavana
(D) Bhikkhu Pannakara
(E) Theravada Bhikkhu
explanation: The article identifies the leader as "the Venerable Bhikkhu Pannakara."
model_output:
The leader of the band of Buddhist monks completing the "Walk for Peace" is:

<final_answer>  
(D) Bhikkhu Pannakara  
</final_answer>

Reasoning:

- Reports identify the group’s leader as **Venerable Bhikkhu Pannakara**, who is the “soft-spoken leader” of the Buddhist monks walking for peace from Fort Worth, Texas to Washington, D.C. ([wlrn.org](https://www.wlrn.org/npr-breaking-news/2026-02-10/buddhist-monks-head-to-dc-to-finish-a-walk-for-peace-that-captivated-millions?utm_source=openai))  
- Other options are incorrect in this context:
  - Mariann Budde is a Washington Episcopal Bishop, not the walking group’s leader ([wboc.com](https://www.wboc.com/news/national/buddhist-monks-head-to-dc-to-finish-a-walk-for-peace-that-captivated-millions/article_ae78360b-9d50-50c2-b089-30cbb4c2fe50.html?utm_source=openai))  
  - Huong Dao is part of the name of the Vipassana Bhavana Center where the monks began, not the walking leader ([altoonamirror.com](https://www.altoonamirror.com/news/national/2026/01/walking-for-peace-buddhist-monks-dog-captivate-americans/?utm_source=openai))  
  - Vipassana Bhavana refers to the center or meditation practice, not a person ([altoonamirror.com](https://www.altoonamirror.com/news/national/2026/01/walking-for-peace-buddhist-monks-dog-captivate-americans/?utm_source=openai))  
  - “Theravada Bhikkhu” is a general term for a monk in the Theravada tradition, not a specific individual leader.

Thus, (D) is the correct choice.
